portfolios.title: Leader — Strategy, Performance & External Relations

budget_latest.note: Cross-cutting — no single budget line. Oversees all portfolios.

statutory_duties.summary: Leader accountable for executive functions and cabinet appointments
statutory_duties.risk: Constitutional role — cannot be cut

statutory_duties.summary: General power of competence
statutory_duties.risk: Enables broad action

known_pressures: LGR transition planning — vesting Apr 2028
CCA governance requirements
Cross-party relationship management

savings_levers.description: Review corporate centre headcount post-2025 election. Multiple interim/consultancy appointments could be made permanent or eliminated.
Review all membership subscriptions, partnership contributions and secondments. Assess value-for-money of each.

portfolios.title: Children & Families
directors: Director of Policy Commissioning and Children's Health
Director of Children's Services Reform and Professional Standards

scrutiny_committee.name: Children, Families and Skills Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to safeguard and promote welfare of children in need, accommodate looked-after children
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot reduce below statutory minimum — Ofsted inspection risk

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to cooperate for children's wellbeing, Local Safeguarding arrangements
statutory_duties.risk: Ofsted will judge inadequate if arrangements weakened

statutory_duties.summary: Corporate parenting principles, local offer for care leavers
statutory_duties.risk: Care leaver entitlements non-negotiable

statutory_duties.summary: Youth offending team provision
statutory_duties.risk: Joint funding with police — reduction needs partner agreement

key_services: Looked after children and foster care
known_pressures: Looked after children numbers rising — 2,100+ (national trend)
Agency social worker costs — £15-20M p.a. at premium rates
Residential placement costs — £4,000-8,000/week per child
Unaccompanied asylum-seeking children (UASC) — 180+, Home Office funding gap
Ofsted inspection due — inadequate rating would trigger intervention

savings_levers.description: Convert top 50 agency social workers to permanent. Agency rates 40-60% premium. Requires competitive salary offer and retention package.
For every child stepped down from residential (£250K/yr) to fostering (£30K/yr) = £220K saving. Target 25-40 step-downs.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Must demonstrate placement meets child's needs — Care Planning Regulations 2010

savings_levers.description: Recruit 50 additional in-house foster carers. In-house costs 60% less than independent fostering agencies (IFAs).
Invest in intensive family support to prevent children entering care. Every child diverted from care = £40-70K annual saving.
Systematic review of Home Office funding claims for UASC. National Transfer Scheme rates often below actual cost — pursue full cost recovery.

portfolios.title: Resources, HR & Property
directors: Noel O'Neill — Interim Director of Finance and Commerce (S151 Officer)
Heloise MacAndrew — Director of Law and Governance (Monitoring Officer)

statutory_duties.summary: S151 officer must ensure proper financial administration
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot remove — statutory officer role

statutory_duties.summary: Annual accounts preparation and external audit
statutory_duties.risk: Legal requirement

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to appoint officers for proper discharge of functions
statutory_duties.risk: Flexible on structure but must maintain core functions

statutory_duties.summary: Births, deaths and marriages registration
statutory_duties.risk: Statutory service — Home Office funded

statutory_duties.summary: Coroner service provision
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot reduce — joint service with other authorities

known_pressures: DSG deficit — £171.4M forecast, rising to £419.9M by 2028/29
Reserves declining 6 consecutive years (£420M → £345M)
Property estate maintenance backlog
Pension fund liabilities
External audit delays (sector-wide)

savings_levers.description: 1,200+ properties. Identify surplus assets for disposal, co-locate services, reduce running costs. Every vacant property costs £30-100K/yr to maintain.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Disposal must follow best consideration requirements (S123 LGA 1972)

savings_levers.description: Support services currently £123M gross (huge). Review internal recharging model, eliminate duplication between central and directorate-level admin.
Cross-council procurement aggregation, framework consolidation, contract renegotiation on expiring contracts. Target 3-5% of addressable spend.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Public Contracts Regulations 2015 — must follow procurement law

savings_levers.description: Automate finance processes (e-invoicing, three-way matching, automated reconciliation). Reduce headcount through natural attrition.

portfolios.title: Adult Social Care

scrutiny_committee.name: Health and Adult Services Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to assess and meet eligible care needs. Wellbeing duty. Prevention duty.
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot cut below assessed eligible needs — judicial review risk

statutory_duties.summary: Best interests decisions, DoLS authorisations
statutory_duties.risk: Deprivation of Liberty — unlawful if not authorised

statutory_duties.summary: Aftercare for detained patients — free, non-means-tested
statutory_duties.risk: s117 aftercare CANNOT be charged for — absolute duty

statutory_duties.summary: Market shaping duty — ensure diverse, sustainable provider market
statutory_duties.risk: If major provider fails, council becomes provider of last resort

known_pressures: Demographic growth — over-65 population rising 2.1% p.a. in Lancashire
National Living Wage impact on provider costs (annual 5-7% uplift)
Care market sustainability — provider failure risk in rural areas
Hospital discharge delays — NHS cost shunt to social care
CHC disputes with NHS Lancashire & South Cumbria ICB — estimated £12M+ unrecovered
CQC inspection regime — new single assessment framework

savings_levers.description: Systematic review of NHS Continuing Healthcare eligibility. LCC is funding care packages that should be NHS-funded. Establish dedicated CHC review team.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: National Framework for NHS CHC 2022 — joint dispute resolution process

savings_levers.description: Invest in short-term reablement to reduce long-term residential placements. Every 1% shift from residential to community saves ~£3M. Current reablement capacity undersized.
Remote monitoring, telecare, digital care planning. Reduces face-to-face visits for stable clients. Technology Enabled Care programme.
Self-directed support is typically 15-20% cheaper than council-commissioned packages. Increase uptake from current ~15% to 25%.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Care Act 2014 s31-33 — must offer DPs to all eligible

savings_levers.description: Strategic commissioning to increase competition, block contract where appropriate, reduce spot purchasing premium. Current HHI suggests healthy competition but pricing above benchmarks.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Public Contracts Regulations 2015, Light Touch Regime for social care

savings_levers.description: Investment in falls prevention, social prescribing, community assets to delay care onset. Every fall prevented saves £30K+ in residential placement. Cross-portfolio with Public Health.

portfolios.title: Health & Wellbeing

scrutiny_committee.name: Health and Adult Services Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Director of Public Health — statutory chief officer. Public health functions.
statutory_duties.risk: DPH is statutory role — cannot remove

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to take steps to improve public health
statutory_duties.risk: Broad duty — discretion on how to deliver

statutory_duties.summary: Health protection and disease control
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot reduce emergency preparedness

known_pressures: Public Health Grant ring-fenced but flat in real terms
Drug and alcohol demand increasing post-COVID
Mental health demand — particularly young people
Health inequalities widening in deprived Lancashire wards

savings_levers.description: Retender drug/alcohol and sexual health contracts at scale. Current contracts may not reflect best value. Benchmark against other county councils.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Public Health Grant conditions — must spend on prescribed functions

savings_levers.description: Every £1 invested in falls prevention saves £3.50 in Adult Social Care. Every £1 in smoking cessation saves £2.80 in NHS/social care. This portfolio is the long-term savings engine.

portfolios.title: Education & Skills

scrutiny_committee.name: Children, Families and Skills Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Ensure sufficient school places, school transport for eligible children
statutory_duties.risk: Home to school transport is major cost driver — statutory distances apply

statutory_duties.summary: SEND — Education Health and Care Plans (EHCPs), local offer
statutory_duties.risk: SEND is the biggest financial risk in education. DSG deficit £171M rising to £419M.

statutory_duties.summary: Early years provision — sufficient childcare, free entitlement
statutory_duties.risk: DfE funded but council has sufficiency duty

statutory_duties.summary: Comprehensive and efficient library service
statutory_duties.risk: Must provide 'comprehensive' service — reductions need consultation

known_pressures: DSG deficit — £171.4M forecast, rising to £419.9M by 2028/29 (CRITICAL)
SEND demand — EHCP numbers rising 10-15% p.a. nationally
SEND transport costs — £40M+ and rising 20%+ p.a.
Library network — 39 branches, high property costs vs digital demand
School place surplus in some areas (demographic decline in East Lancashire)

savings_levers.description: SEND transport is the fastest-growing cost. Route optimisation, personal travel budgets, independent travel training for older students. Benchmark against best-performing councils.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Education Act 1996 s508B — must provide transport to eligible children. SEND Code of Practice.

savings_levers.description: 39 libraries. Community-managed model for low-use branches, digital-first strategy, co-location with other services. Requires public consultation.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Public Libraries and Museums Act 1964 — must consult and maintain 'comprehensive' service. Judicial review risk (see Brent 2011).

savings_levers.description: Invest in mainstream school SEND support to reduce EHCP demand. Every child kept in mainstream vs special school saves £25-40K/yr in placement + transport.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Children and Families Act 2014 — parental preference for special school must be considered

savings_levers.description: Review discretionary transport (post-16, denominational). Route optimisation for statutory transport.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Statutory distances: 2 miles (under 8), 3 miles (8-16). Discretionary policies can be changed with consultation.

portfolios.title: Economic Development & Growth
directors: Simon Lawrence — Director of Growth and Regeneration

scrutiny_committee.name: Environment, Economic Growth and Transport Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Power to promote economic wellbeing (now general power of competence)
statutory_duties.risk: Discretionary — can shape as needed

known_pressures: Lancashire productivity gap vs national average
Town centre decline in East Lancashire
Post-levelling-up fund uncertainty

savings_levers.description: Review all economic development programmes for outcomes vs cost. Discontinue low-impact schemes. Focus investment on highest-ROI areas.
Dedicated external funding team to capture UKSPF, Levelling Up, CCA investment. Every £1 in matched funding reduces council contribution.

portfolios.title: Highways & Transport
directors: Matt Townsend — Director of Highways and Transport

scrutiny_committee.name: Environment, Economic Growth and Transport Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Duty to maintain highways — non-delegable duty of care
statutory_duties.risk: Failure to maintain = liability for injury claims. Largest claims area.

statutory_duties.summary: Network management duty — keep traffic moving
statutory_duties.risk: Must have network management plan

statutory_duties.act: New Roads and Street Works Act 1991
statutory_duties.summary: Coordinate utility works, recover reinstatement costs
statutory_duties.risk: Revenue opportunity — s59 recharges to utility companies

statutory_duties.summary: Lead local flood authority duties
statutory_duties.risk: Must maintain flood risk strategy

known_pressures: Maintenance backlog — estimated £200M+ to bring network to good condition
Climate change — increased flood and storm damage
Utility company damage to roads — s59 enforcement opportunity
Street lighting energy costs (mitigated by LED programme)
Bus service funding post-BSIP

savings_levers.description: NRSWA 1991 s59 allows councils to charge utility companies for excessive road damage. Many councils under-recover. Establish dedicated enforcement team.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: NRSWA 1991 — clear legal basis. Utility companies may dispute but case law supports councils.

savings_levers.description: Every £1 spent on preventive maintenance saves £4-8 in reactive repair. Shift budget from filling potholes to surface treatments and micro-asphalt.
LED conversion programme savings. Part-night lighting (midnight-6am dimming) in low-risk areas. Already partially implemented.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Must maintain lighting on classified roads — discretionary on unclassified

savings_levers.description: Permit fees for street works, advertising on highway assets, expanded parking charges. Review fee levels against statutory maximums.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Fees must not exceed cost of service (except where statute permits surplus)

portfolios.title: Rural Affairs, Environment & Communities

scrutiny_committee.name: Environment, Economic Growth and Transport Scrutiny Committee

statutory_duties.summary: Waste disposal authority — must arrange disposal of controlled waste
statutory_duties.risk: Cannot stop waste disposal — statutory duty. Largest single contract (Lancashire Renewables £71M).

statutory_duties.summary: Biodiversity duty, waste strategy
statutory_duties.risk: New duties — still clarifying scope

statutory_duties.summary: Emergency planning and resilience
statutory_duties.risk: Category 1 responder — must maintain emergency plans

statutory_duties.summary: Trading standards enforcement
statutory_duties.risk: Can reduce but not eliminate — doorstep crime, food safety

known_pressures: Waste disposal contract (Lancashire Renewables) — £71M+ and indexed to inflation
Environment Act 2021 — new biodiversity and waste duties
Climate action commitments
Rural service delivery costs — sparse population

savings_levers.description: Lancashire Renewables contract is single biggest external contract (£71M). Review indexation terms, performance deductions, recycling credits. Even 5% saving = £3.5M.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: PFI/PPP contract terms may limit renegotiation scope. Legal review of break clauses needed.

savings_levers.description: Shift from reactive complaints to intelligence-led enforcement. Digital-first consumer advice. Regional collaboration.
Review countryside properties and access points. Community transfer where appropriate.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Countryside and Rights of Way Act 2000 — must maintain definitive map

portfolios.title: Data, Technology, Customer & Efficiency

budget_latest.note: Shared with Resources portfolio — ICT and digital within Central Services budget

statutory_duties.summary: Data protection and information governance
statutory_duties.risk: ICO enforcement — fines up to £17.5M or 4% of turnover

statutory_duties.summary: Respond to FOI requests within 20 working days
statutory_duties.risk: Must comply but can charge for complex requests

known_pressures: Legacy systems requiring replacement (Liquid Logic, SAP)
Cyber security threats increasing
Customer expectations for digital self-service
AI and automation opportunities not yet captured

savings_levers.description: Every transaction shifted from phone/face-to-face to digital saves £5-10 per interaction. Target 80% digital for routine services. Invest in self-service portals.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Must maintain accessibility for digitally excluded — Equality Act 2010

savings_levers.description: Multiple overlapping systems across directorates. Consolidate onto fewer platforms, eliminate duplicate licences.
Automate routine processes: FOI triage, invoice matching, customer routing, report generation. Each automated process saves 0.5-2 FTE.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Must ensure AI decisions are fair and auditable — public sector equality duty

savings_levers.description: Cross-council procurement aggregation, category management, e-procurement. Target 3-5% savings on £400M+ addressable spend across all portfolios. This portfolio enables savings everywhere else.
savings_levers.legal_constraints: Procurement Act 2023 (from Oct 2024) — new framework. Must follow procedures.

s151_officer.name: Noel O'Neill
s151_officer.title: Interim Director of Finance and Commerce
s151_officer.responsibility: Proper administration of council finances. Must report if budget is at risk of being unbalanced (s114 notice).

monitoring_officer.name: Heloise MacAndrew
monitoring_officer.title: Director of Law and Governance
monitoring_officer.responsibility: Ensures council acts lawfully. Must report if any proposal or decision contravenes law.

executive_directors.name: Jacqui Old CBE
executive_directors.title: Executive Director of Education and Children's Services

executive_directors.name: Phil Green
executive_directors.title: Executive Director of Growth, Environment, Transport and Health

officer_delegation.description: Officer decides under scheme of delegation
officer_delegation.authority: Constitution Part 3, Schedule 5 — Scheme of Delegation to Officers
officer_delegation.reporting: Reported to next Cabinet meeting for noting
officer_delegation.suitable_for: Operational decisions within approved budget and policy framework

cabinet_member_decision.authority: Constitution Part 4, Section B — Individual Cabinet Member Decisions
cabinet_member_decision.constraints: Must not be contrary to approved budget or policy framework. Subject to call-in.

cabinet_decision.authority: Constitution Part 4, Section A — Cabinet Decision-Making
cabinet_decision.suitable_for: Key Decisions, new policies, budget virements, contract awards above threshold

full_council.schedule: Quarterly + Annual Budget + Extraordinary as needed

urgency.authority: Constitution Part 4, Section D — Urgency Procedure
urgency.requirements: Chief Executive AND Chair of relevant scrutiny committee must both agree to waive Forward Plan and/or call-in

scrutiny_committees.name: Budget and Finance Scrutiny Committee
scrutiny_committees.powers: Can require any cabinet member or officer to attend. Can call-in cabinet decisions. Can refer matters to Full Council.

scrutiny_committees.name: Health and Adult Services Scrutiny Committee
scrutiny_committees.powers: Statutory health scrutiny power — can require NHS bodies to attend. Can refer NHS service changes to Secretary of State.

scrutiny_committees.name: Children, Families and Skills Scrutiny Committee
scrutiny_committees.powers: Can require any cabinet member or officer to attend. Education scrutiny including academies.

scrutiny_committees.name: Environment, Economic Growth and Transport Scrutiny Committee
scrutiny_committees.powers: Can require any cabinet member or officer to attend. Flood scrutiny powers.

political_arithmetic.callin_note: Opposition has 31 members — call-in (5 signatures) is easily achievable
political_arithmetic.full_council_override: Reform majority means any Full Council referral would be won 53-31
